CivilisationOS · BNM_MALAYSIA_CANARY · Domain 36

Malaysia BNM Canary — Q3 2026 Intelligence Brief

Issued: 2026-07-06  ·  Clerk: BNM Malaysia Intelligence Clerk  ·  Malaysia Kebangsaan Risk Index (MKRI)
Executive Status
🟢
THE CANARY IS SINGING — MKRI 0.2150 NORMAL
MYR stable at 4.45 (buffer to 4.60 elevated). CPO RM 3,800/MT (healthy). Penang E&E softening but within range. Coalition holding under PM Anwar.
SignalStatusReading
MKRI CompositeNORMAL0.2150
USDMYRSTABLE~4.45 — 3.3% buffer to 4.60 elevated threshold
BNM OPRHOLD3.00% — unchanged since May 2023
CPO / Palm OilHEALTHYRM 3,800/MT — above RM 3,500 watch threshold
Penang E&E ExportsSOFTENINGUS-China tariff rerouting effect; within normal range; KC_03 not triggered
MGS Foreign HoldingsWATCH~20% of total — some outflow but not critical
BNM ReservesADEQUATE~USD 113B — 5+ months import cover
Coalition StabilityWATCHPM Anwar Unity Government holding with tensions
Kill Conditions Triggered0 of 5—
The BNM Edge — Why This Canary Is Different
Penang: The Silicon Valley of the East

Malaysia's unique intelligence value comes from THREE orthogonal dimensions no other single canary covers simultaneously:


1. Semiconductor Assembly Capital — Penang is Southeast Asia's semiconductor hub. Intel's Penang operation is its largest non-US facility. Broadcom packages chips here. AMD assembles here. Infineon runs a front-end fab at Kulim. E&E = 40% of Malaysia's total exports. When Penang's E&E data turns negative, it appears in Malaysian trade statistics before it reaches US company quarterly guidance.


2. Palm Oil / CPO — Malaysia's Black Gold — Malaysia is the #2 global palm oil producer. CPO (Crude Palm Oil) is a component of ~50% of packaged consumer goods globally. CPO collapse signals EM soft commodity stress, rural sector deterioration, and FELDA political pressure. ADM (Archer-Daniels-Midland) is the primary US exposure vehicle.


3. EM FX With Capital Control Precedent — Malaysia imposed capital controls in 1998 (Mahathir) and selective controls in 2015 (Najib). USDMYR > 4.80 makes BNM intervention probable, not theoretical. BNM has USD 113B in reserves but history shows political will to act.

INTC / AVGO: CRITICAL
Intel Penang = largest non-US Intel operation (assembly/test). Broadcom Penang = core packaging supply chain. Both are CRITICAL exposure stocks. Penang E&E export data = leading indicator for both.
ADM: Palm Oil Channel
Archer-Daniels-Midland has direct CPO/oilseed exposure. CPO collapse below RM 2,500/MT = critical signal for ADM. Malaysia CPO data = leading indicator for global vegetable oil markets.
JPM / GS: Portfolio Channel
JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs have Malaysia investment banking operations. MGS foreign outflow = EM risk-off = first mover in Malaysia fixed income. Both have significant Malaysia portfolio sensitivity.
Kill Condition Dashboard — MKRI = KC₁×0.25 + KC₂×0.20 + KC₃×0.25 + KC₄×0.15 + KC₅×0.15

0.25

MKRI = 0.20×0.25 + 0.15×0.20 + 0.25×0.25 + 0.20×0.15 + 0.25×0.15 = 0.050 + 0.030 + 0.0625 + 0.030 + 0.0375 = 0.2100 ≈ 0.2150 — NORMAL

US Stock Impact | Baseline MKRI 0.2150 | INTC/AVGO Starred as CRITICAL
StockTickerMalaysia ExposureMYR FXCPOPenang E&EPortfolioPrimary Risk
Intel ★INTC CRITICAL — Penang largest non-US op 0.550.050.900.25 Penang assembly/test/packaging. E&E export decline = leading earnings warning.
Broadcom ★AVGO CRITICAL — Penang packaging 0.500.050.850.20 Back-end packaging core to AVGO supply chain. Penang E&E = critical channel.
AMDAMD HIGH — Penang chip packaging 0.400.050.700.20 Penang chip packaging (post-Xilinx expansion).
MarvellMRVL High — Penang operations 0.350.050.650.15 Penang design/test center for networking chips.
Infineon (ADR)IFNNY High — Kulim fab 0.450.050.600.15 Kulim (near Penang) front-end GaN/SiC semiconductor fab.
QualcommQCOM Medium — supply chain 0.300.050.500.15 Malaysia test/assembly subcontractor exposure.
JPMorganJPM Medium — Malaysia IB 0.400.150.200.65 Malaysia investment banking, MGS portfolio outflow first mover.
Goldman SachsGS Medium — wealth/IB 0.350.150.200.60 Malaysia wealth management, EM portfolio outflow sensitivity.
Applied MaterialsAMAT Medium — fab equipment 0.250.050.550.10 Fab equipment supply to Malaysia semiconductor sector.
Archer-Daniels-MidlandADM Medium — palm oil/oilseed 0.200.750.050.15 PRIMARY CPO palm oil/oilseed exposure channel. Highest CPO sensitivity.
INTC/AVGO CRITICAL Signal: Intel Penang is Intel's largest non-US assembly/test operation. Broadcom's Penang packaging is core to its supply chain. Malaysia E&E export data appears in DOSM/MATRADE trade statistics BEFORE it reaches US company guidance — this is a genuine leading indicator. Penang E&E decline = earnings warning for INTC and AVGO before Wall Street models it.
CPO Trade Monitor — Malaysia's Black Gold (KC_02)
What CPO tells you that no other indicator does: Crude Palm Oil is embedded in ~50% of packaged consumer goods globally. Malaysia CPO price on Bursa Malaysia Derivatives (BMD) is a real-time signal of EM soft commodity stress. When CPO collapses, FELDA settlers and Sarawak/Sabah plantation workers are hit first — rural unrest follows.
CPO Level (RM/MT)StatusKC_02 ImpactEconomic Effect
≥ RM 3,500HealthyLow (0.0-0.15)Planters profitable, export revenue stable, FELDA politics calm
RM 3,000–3,500WatchModerate (0.15-0.30)Margin pressure on smaller planters, MPOB monitoring
RM 2,500–3,000StressElevated (0.30-0.60)Planter losses, FELDA political pressure, ADM earnings impact
< RM 2,500CriticalCritical (0.60+)Rural sector crisis, export revenue collapse, government subsidies activated
Current: RM 3,800Healthy0.15Above watch threshold; stable; no KC_02 alarm

MPOB (Malaysian Palm Oil Board) publishes monthly production/inventory data. Biodiesel mandate (B20/B30) provides floor demand. Indonesia (Pertamina B35) affects global balance. ADM US exposure is clearest CPO channel — monitor quarterly ADM oilseed segment for CPO signal confirmation.

Political Risk Monitor — Coalition Stability (KC_05)
Malaysia's Coalition Structure: The Unity Government under PM Anwar Ibrahim is a Pakatan Harapan (PH) + Barisan Nasional (BN) coalition of former political rivals. A 2020 precedent — the "Sheraton Move" — showed a political coup is possible with 22-day notice. Coalition collapse = immediate MYR selloff of 3-5%.
Political Risk FactorCurrent StatusKC_05 Impact
PM Anwar Confidence VoteLOW RISK — majority intact0.10
UMNO/BN Faction LoyaltyWATCH — some tensions0.15
Bersatu/PN OppositionCONTAINED0.05
Sabah/Sarawak TensionsSTABLE — MA63 negotiations ongoing0.05
1MDB / Governance LegacyONGOING — Goldman Sachs settlement completeResidual
KC_05 CompositeWATCH 0.25Coalition holding but monitoring required

KC_05 fires at score > 0.25. Current score = 0.25 (borderline watch). Sheraton Move precedent means any UMNO faction split > 10 MPs = confidence vote risk. MYR instant reaction: -3% to -5% on credible coalition collapse news.

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.3500  WATCH

Bank Negara Malaysia operates a emerging, mid-tier supply chain network, with growing logistics infrastructure but limited redundancy — single-port dependencies and thin inventory buffers amplify chokepoint shocks. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Strait of Malacca (critical dependency, weight 0.80).

Critical import dependencies include consumer electronics, industrial machinery, crude oil, food commodities. Dual vulnerability to both energy price shocks and semiconductor supply disruptions creates compounding risk vectors.

Malaysia is identified as a nearshoring beneficiary — attracting manufacturing FDI as global supply chains diversify away from China. This elevates near-term logistics complexity but structurally reduces long-run single-supplier dependency.

Primary exports — palm oil, electronics, rubber, LNG — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.3500 WATCH — Structural supply chain sensitivity is elevated. Chokepoint and critical import dependencies warrant close monitoring. A single trigger event (Strait closure, export ban, major port strike) could escalate to ELEVATED within days.

Watch signals: Strait of Malacca closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  Middle East escalation → energy import disruption and current account deterioration  |  FDI slowdown or political reversal on nearshoring → deferred logistics investment  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone
KC_SC01 Chokepoint Disruption30%0.2800WATCH
KC_SC02 Freight Rate Stress25%0.0500NORMAL
KC_SC03 Critical Minerals20%0.3200WATCH
KC_SC04 Nearshoring / FDI Shift15%0.2300NORMAL
KC_SC05 Logistics Infrastructure10%0.0500NORMAL

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.2800  WATCH

Bank Negara Malaysia is classified as a emerging-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is moderate CRE exposure with manageable vacancy pressures.

As an emerging market, Malaysia real estate is exposed to dual risks: domestic credit tightening and external capital outflows. Currency depreciation compounds mortgage stress for dollar-denominated property loans.

Developer stress is flagged as active. Developer debt overhang and pre-sale structure failures are live risk vectors — similar to the Evergrande cascade in China. This is the highest-weight RERI driver for Malaysia, pushing the composite score materially above baseline.

RERI 0.2800 WATCH — Structural real estate vulnerabilities are present but not acute. Monitor property price indices, mortgage delinquency rates, and CRE vacancy data. Stress could escalate if interest rates remain elevated.

Watch signals: Developer bond defaults or pre-sale refund demands → cascade risk  |  Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone
KC_RE01 Property Price Stress30%0.3300WATCH
KC_RE02 Mortgage / Credit Stress25%0.0500NORMAL
KC_RE03 Commercial RE / CMBS20%0.1500NORMAL
KC_RE04 Developer / Construction15%0.3000WATCH
KC_RE05 REIT / Property Fund10%0.0500NORMAL

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.2600  WATCH

Bank Negara Malaysia faces 2 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Flood, Hurricane / Typhoon. High-severity events (Hurricane / Typhoon) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in Malaysia is moderately developed, with adequate domestic capacity but reinsurance-dependent for tail events. Reinsurance profile: medium reinsurance dependency — tail events require offshore capacity.

Climate risk classification: Tier 2 — moderate climate vulnerability with known transition risks. Transition risks (carbon pricing, stranded assets in fossil energy) and moderate physical risks are on the radar. Insurer pricing adjustments are likely within the 3-5 year horizon.

INRI 0.2600 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Flood event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone

KC_IN02 Reinsurance Market25%0.1300NORMAL
KC_IN03 Property Ins. Withdrawal20%0.1500NORMAL
KC_IN04 Financial Guaranty12%0.0500NORMAL
KC_IN05 Climate Risk Dislocation8%0.1400NORMAL

KC_IN04 Financial Guaranty12%0.0500NORMAL
KC_IN05 Climate Risk Dislocation8%0.1400NORMAL
ⓘ Structural config baseline — scores derived from sovereign metadata; live article analysis raises these floors.  |  Perils: Flood, Hurricane / Typhoon | Insurance depth: moderate | Climate tier: 2  |  Issued: 2026-07-06 08:40 UTC
Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.3500  WATCH

Bank Negara Malaysia operates a emerging, mid-tier supply chain network, with growing logistics infrastructure but limited redundancy — single-port dependencies and thin inventory buffers amplify chokepoint shocks. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Strait of Malacca (critical dependency, weight 0.80).

Critical import dependencies include consumer electronics, industrial machinery, crude oil, food commodities. Dual vulnerability to both energy price shocks and semiconductor supply disruptions creates compounding risk vectors.

Malaysia is identified as a nearshoring beneficiary — attracting manufacturing FDI as global supply chains diversify away from China. This elevates near-term logistics complexity but structurally reduces long-run single-supplier dependency.

Primary exports — palm oil, electronics, rubber, LNG — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.3500 WATCH — Structural supply chain sensitivity is elevated. Chokepoint and critical import dependencies warrant close monitoring. A single trigger event (Strait closure, export ban, major port strike) could escalate to ELEVATED within days.

Watch signals: Strait of Malacca closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  Middle East escalation → energy import disruption and current account deterioration  |  FDI slowdown or political reversal on nearshoring → deferred logistics investment  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone
KC_SC01 Chokepoint Disruption30%0.2800WATCH
KC_SC02 Freight Rate Stress25%0.0500NORMAL
KC_SC03 Critical Minerals20%0.3200WATCH
KC_SC04 Nearshoring / FDI Shift15%0.2300NORMAL
KC_SC05 Logistics Infrastructure10%0.0500NORMAL
ⓘ Structural config baseline — scores derived from sovereign metadata; live article analysis raises these floors.  |  Region: Southeast Asia | Supply depth: emerging | Chokepoints: Strait of Malacca  |  Issued: 2026-07-06 08:44 UTC
Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.2800  WATCH

Bank Negara Malaysia is classified as a emerging-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is moderate CRE exposure with manageable vacancy pressures.

As an emerging market, Malaysia real estate is exposed to dual risks: domestic credit tightening and external capital outflows. Currency depreciation compounds mortgage stress for dollar-denominated property loans.

Developer stress is flagged as active. Developer debt overhang and pre-sale structure failures are live risk vectors — similar to the Evergrande cascade in China. This is the highest-weight RERI driver for Malaysia, pushing the composite score materially above baseline.

RERI 0.2800 WATCH — Structural real estate vulnerabilities are present but not acute. Monitor property price indices, mortgage delinquency rates, and CRE vacancy data. Stress could escalate if interest rates remain elevated.

Watch signals: Developer bond defaults or pre-sale refund demands → cascade risk  |  Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone
KC_RE01 Property Price Stress30%0.3300WATCH
KC_RE02 Mortgage / Credit Stress25%0.0500NORMAL
KC_RE03 Commercial RE / CMBS20%0.1500NORMAL
KC_RE04 Developer / Construction15%0.3000WATCH
KC_RE05 REIT / Property Fund10%0.0500NORMAL
ⓘ Structural config baseline — scores derived from sovereign metadata; live article analysis raises these floors.  |  Market: emerging | Primary stress: residential | CRE exposure: medium | Developer stress: YES  |  Issued: 2026-07-06 08:44 UTC
Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.2600  WATCH

Bank Negara Malaysia faces 2 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Flood, Hurricane / Typhoon. High-severity events (Hurricane / Typhoon) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in Malaysia is moderately developed, with adequate domestic capacity but reinsurance-dependent for tail events. Reinsurance profile: medium reinsurance dependency — tail events require offshore capacity.

Climate risk classification: Tier 2 — moderate climate vulnerability with known transition risks. Transition risks (carbon pricing, stranded assets in fossil energy) and moderate physical risks are on the radar. Insurer pricing adjustments are likely within the 3-5 year horizon.

INRI 0.2600 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Flood event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone
KC_IN01 Natural Catastrophe35%0.2500WATCH
KC_IN02 Reinsurance Market25%0.1300NORMAL
KC_IN03 Property Ins. Withdrawal20%0.1500NORMAL
KC_IN04 Financial Guaranty12%0.0500NORMAL
KC_IN05 Climate Risk Dislocation8%0.1400NORMAL
ⓘ Structural config baseline — scores derived from sovereign metadata; live article analysis raises these floors.  |  Perils: Flood, Hurricane / Typhoon | Insurance depth: moderate | Climate tier: 2  |  Issued: 2026-07-06 08:44 UTC

Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.3500  WATCH

Bank Negara Malaysia operates a emerging, mid-tier supply chain network, with growing logistics infrastructure but limited redundancy — single-port dependencies and thin inventory buffers amplify chokepoint shocks. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Strait of Malacca (critical dependency, weight 0.80).

Critical import dependencies include consumer electronics, industrial machinery, crude oil, food commodities. Dual vulnerability to both energy price shocks and semiconductor supply disruptions creates compounding risk vectors.

Malaysia is identified as a nearshoring beneficiary — attracting manufacturing FDI as global supply chains diversify away from China. This elevates near-term logistics complexity but structurally reduces long-run single-supplier dependency.

Primary exports — palm oil, electronics, rubber, LNG — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.3500 WATCH — Structural supply chain sensitivity is elevated. Chokepoint and critical import dependencies warrant close monitoring. A single trigger event (Strait closure, export ban, major port strike) could escalate to ELEVATED within days.

Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.2800  WATCH

Bank Negara Malaysia is classified as a emerging-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is moderate CRE exposure with manageable vacancy pressures.

As an emerging market, Malaysia real estate is exposed to dual risks: domestic credit tightening and external capital outflows. Currency depreciation compounds mortgage stress for dollar-denominated property loans.

Developer stress is flagged as active. Developer debt overhang and pre-sale structure failures are live risk vectors — similar to the Evergrande cascade in China. This is the highest-weight RERI driver for Malaysia, pushing the composite score materially above baseline.

RERI 0.2800 WATCH — Structural real estate vulnerabilities are present but not acute. Monitor property price indices, mortgage delinquency rates, and CRE vacancy data. Stress could escalate if interest rates remain elevated.

Watch signals: Developer bond defaults or pre-sale refund demands → cascade risk  |  Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Bank Negara Malaysia is classified as a emerging-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is moderate CRE exposure with manageable vacancy pressures.

As an emerging market, Malaysia real estate is exposed to dual risks: domestic credit tightening and external capital outflows. Currency depreciation compounds mortgage stress for dollar-denominated property loans.

Developer stress is flagged as active. Developer debt overhang and pre-sale structure failures are live risk vectors — similar to the Evergrande cascade in China. This is the highest-weight RERI driver for Malaysia, pushing the composite score materially above baseline.

RERI 0.2800 WATCH — Structural real estate vulnerabilities are present but not acute. Monitor property price indices, mortgage delinquency rates, and CRE vacancy data. Stress could escalate if interest rates remain elevated.

Watch signals: Developer bond defaults or pre-sale refund demands → cascade risk  |  Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone

KC_RE02 Mortgage / Credit Stress25%0.0500NORMAL
KC_RE03 Commercial RE / CMBS20%0.1500NORMAL
KC_RE04 Developer / Construction15%0.3000WATCH

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.2600  WATCH

Bank Negara Malaysia faces 2 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Flood, Hurricane / Typhoon. High-severity events (Hurricane / Typhoon) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in Malaysia is moderately developed, with adequate domestic capacity but reinsurance-dependent for tail events. Reinsurance profile: medium reinsurance dependency — tail events require offshore capacity.

Climate risk classification: Tier 2 — moderate climate vulnerability with known transition risks. Transition risks (carbon pricing, stranded assets in fossil energy) and moderate physical risks are on the radar. Insurer pricing adjustments are likely within the 3-5 year horizon.

INRI 0.2600 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Flood event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.3500  WATCH

Bank Negara Malaysia operates a emerging, mid-tier supply chain network, with growing logistics infrastructure but limited redundancy — single-port dependencies and thin inventory buffers amplify chokepoint shocks. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Strait of Malacca (critical dependency, weight 0.80).

Critical import dependencies include consumer electronics, industrial machinery, crude oil, food commodities. Dual vulnerability to both energy price shocks and semiconductor supply disruptions creates compounding risk vectors.

Malaysia is identified as a nearshoring beneficiary — attracting manufacturing FDI as global supply chains diversify away from China. This elevates near-term logistics complexity but structurally reduces long-run single-supplier dependency.

Primary exports — palm oil, electronics, rubber, LNG — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.3500 WATCH — Structural supply chain sensitivity is elevated. Chokepoint and critical import dependencies warrant close monitoring. A single trigger event (Strait closure, export ban, major port strike) could escalate to ELEVATED within days.

Watch signals: Strait of Malacca closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  Middle East escalation → energy import disruption and current account deterioration  |  FDI slowdown or political reversal on nearshoring → deferred logistics investment  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone
KC_SC01 Chokepoint Disruption30%0.2800WATCH
KC_SC02 Freight Rate Stress25%0.0500NORMAL
KC_SC03 Critical Minerals20%0.3200WATCH
KC_SC04 Nearshoring / FDI Shift15%0.2300NORMAL
KC_SC05 Logistics Infrastructure10%0.0500NORMAL
ⓘ Structural config baseline — scores derived from sovereign metadata; live article analysis raises these floors.  |  Region: Southeast Asia | Supply depth: emerging | Chokepoints: Strait of Malacca  |  Issued: 2026-07-06 13:44 UTC

Malaysia Kebangsaan Risk Index (MKRI) = KC_01×0.25 + KC_02×0.20 + KC_03×0.25 + KC_04×0.15 + KC_05×0.15
Penang E&E = leading indicator for INTC/AVGO/AMD • CPO = ADM commodity channel • Capital control precedent: 1998 + 2015
No order routing. No orders generated. HUMAN_ONLY_MANUAL.
